Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to analyze a process event log identify recurring activities within the same case that have different contextual meanings rename them to reflect these differences present the revised log and justify the naming choices

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Event Log Lets look at each case and identify recurring activities

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case C001 Verify Documents 1 Context Agent checks identity documents Initial check Call Customer 1 Context Verification incomplete agent calls to request missing docs Verify Documents 2 Context Agent re checks documents now that ID scan is provided Re verification after missing docs submitted Recurring Verify Documents

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case C002 Verify Documents 1 Context Identity verification Initial check Call Customer 1 Context Outbound call to clarify account discrepancy Adjust Account Call Customer 2 Context Follow up call to confirm adjustments made Confirmation call Recurring Call Customer

Name: Pattern Recognition - NE
Text: Case C003 Verify Documents 1 Context Physical verification of ID Initial check Call Customer 1 Context Customer stepped out Officer calls to have them return Adjust Account Verify Documents 2 Context Re verify docs after account adjustment Re verification Recurring Verify Documents Call Customer

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Correction on C003 C003 only has Call Customer once Customer stepped out Officer calls to have them return So Call Customer appears twice only in C002

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Summary of Recurring Activities Verify Documents Appears twice in C001 and C003 First time Initial check of identity documents Second time Re checking or Re verifying after a change missing docs provided or account adjusted Call Customer appears twice only in C002

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Determine Contextual Meanings and New Names Verify Documents First occurrence Initial document verification New Name Initial Document Verification Second occurrence Re verification after an update New Name Document Re verification

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Call Customer First occurrence Outbound call to resolve a blocking issue or get missing info New Name Call Customer for Clarification or similar Second occurrence Follow up call confirming the resolution New Name Call Customer for Confirmation

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets keep the naming convention consistent changing it to Call Customer for Clarification and Call Customer for Confirmation is clean and clear

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait lets look closely at the recurring activity in each case In C001 Verify Documents is the one that repeats In C002 Call Customer is the one that repeats In C003 Verify Documents is the one that repeats

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I will rename all instances of the recurring activities to reflect their specific contexts globally for consistency

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Initial Document Verification Re verify Documents after Update Call Customer to Request Info Call Customer for Clarification Call Customer for Confirmation Call Customer to Recall

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Context based is better The prompt says rename them to reflect their context and highlight their roles in the process flow

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Explain why Verify Documents was split the first instance is a standard initial check and the second instance happens after an intervening event and represents a re check of the newly updated state

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Explain why Call Customer was split in C002 the first call is to identify or resolve an issue while the second is to close the loop by confirmation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Did I miss any recurring activities C001 Verify x2 C002 Call x2 C003 Verify x2 No other activities repeat

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Grouping them into Call Customer to Request Info versus Call Customer to Confirm Resolution is excellent It shows that despite different specific reasons the process role of the first call is to unblock the process by requesting something whereas the second call is a confirmation
